sanskrit:
विहाय कामान्यः सर्वान्पुमांश्चरति निःस्पृहः।
निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः स शान्तिमधिगच्छति॥
transliteration: vihāya kāmān yaḥ sarvān pumāṃś carati niḥspṛhaḥ | nirmamo nirahaṅkāraḥ sa śāntim adhigacchati ||
literal_meaning: Having abandoned all desires, the person who moves without craving, without ‘mine’, without ‘I’, attains peace.
contextual_meaning: Lasting inner peace is not the absence of desire but the redirection of desire away from self-referent craving toward self-transcendent service; ego-proprietorship is the final layer to surrender.
psychological_state: Post-egoic equanimity; secure-functioning secure-attachment to the Whole rather than to objects.
trigger_scenario: You just handed in your resignation, walked out of the office, and realize you no longer need the promotion you once craved—your mind is quiet for the first time in years.
anchor_text: Serve without claiming ownership or seeking personal reward.
